# Introduction


Write your Markdown as you would any other Quarto project. Main body text looks like this paragraph: it spans the standard content width, leaving space on the exterior side of the page for marginal notes.




## Technical writing

All of Quarto's normal features  [*See* Quarto documentation on [technical writing](https://quarto.org/docs/visual-editor/technical.html).]{.column-margin} are available for technical writing.
Here is a sentence that requires a traditional footnote at the bottom of the page[^1]. 

[^1]: See? This footnote stays firmly anchored at the bottom of the page or screen, just like you requested.

Next, let's look at how a figure behaves. We want the caption to remain directly beneath the image rather than floating into the margin.

![A beautiful placeholder image representing structured layouts.](https://neelsmith.quarto.pub/personal/posts/imgs/filled-w-grief.png){#fig-placeholder}

As seen in @fig-placeholder, the figure spans the main text width, and its caption stays cleanly underneath.


## Citing sources

Follow standard Quarto practices to cite bibliography.[*See* [Quarto documentation on citations](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/citations.html).]{.column-margin} `_quarto.yml` configures a bibliographic source file with the line `bibliography: references.bib`. Use reference IDs to cite sources. For example, including a citation like `[@tufte2001]` will produce this citation in your text [@tufte2001]. In both HTML and PDF output, citations are linked to full bibliographic information in the [appended bibliography](./references.qmd).





## Adding marginal notes

This project's configuration lets you add marginal notes in a `column-margin` class. Place the note in square brackets followed by `{.column-margin}`. To align the note horizontally with a specific passage of the main body text, embed the note in the text at the point you want it to align with. For example, this markup:

```
A paragraph [The content of your **marginal note**.]{.column-margin} 
with an aligned marginal note. The paragraph text 
continues to flow normally in the main text block
```


produces this:

> A **paragraph** [The content of your **marginal note**.]{.column-margin} with an aligned marginal note. The paragraph text continues to flow normally in the main text block.
